notes: Antarctica has no permanent civilian population or single everyday language, so this profile intentionally carries no compatibility languages.

evidence.title: Britannica: Antarctica
evidence.note: Britannica explains that Antarctica has no permanent residents.

evidence.title: National Geographic: Antarctica facts
evidence.note: National Geographic describes Antarctica as lacking a permanent settled population.

notes: Bouvet Island is uninhabited, so it should not imply any language compatibility score.

evidence.title: Britannica: Bouvet Island
evidence.note: Britannica describes Bouvet Island as uninhabited.

evidence.title: Norwegian Polar Institute: Bouvetoya
evidence.note: Norway's Polar Institute describes Bouvetoya as a protected, uninhabited dependency.

notes: Western Sahara should score Arabic as the main formal language and Hassaniyya as a strong everyday spoken match.

evidence.title: Britannica: Western Sahara
evidence.note: Britannica discusses Arabic and Hassaniya/Hassaniyya usage in Western Sahara.

evidence.title: Britannica: Sahrawi
evidence.note: Britannica describes Hassaniyya Arabic as central to Sahrawi identity and everyday speech.

notes: Guinea should score French as official and give strong everyday credit to Fulah/Pular. Guinea Kpelle should also count directly because it exists in the picker catalog.

evidence.title: Britannica: Guinea
evidence.note: Britannica identifies French as the official language of Guinea.

evidence.title: Britannica: People of Guinea
evidence.note: Britannica discusses Fulah/Pular as one of the principal languages of Guinea.

notes: South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands have no permanent settled population, but English is the practical administrative language for staff, officials, and expedition-style visitors.

evidence.title: SGSSI official site
evidence.note: The official government site describes South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands as having no permanent residents and serves the territory in English.

evidence.title: Britannica: South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands
evidence.note: Britannica describes the territory as lacking a permanent population.

notes: Heard Island and McDonald Islands are uninhabited, so no language compatibility should be assigned.

evidence.title: Australian Antarctic Program: Heard and McDonald Islands
evidence.note: Australia's Antarctic program describes the islands as uninhabited.

evidence.title: Britannica: Heard and McDonald Islands
evidence.note: Britannica describes the islands as having no permanent population.

notes: The British Indian Ocean Territory does not have a normal settled civilian population, so it should not imply a language score for travelers.

evidence.title: Britannica: British Indian Ocean Territory
evidence.note: Britannica explains the territory's lack of a normal permanent civilian population.

evidence.title: BIOT Administration
evidence.note: The territory's official administration site reflects its special non-tourist, non-settled status.

notes: Nauru should score English as fully compatible for now. Nauruan is a real local language but is not currently represented in the picker catalog, so it should be picked up in the final leftover-language reconciliation pass if added.

evidence.title: Britannica: Nauru
evidence.note: Britannica describes English as central in government and public life in Nauru while also noting Nauruan.

evidence.title: Republic of Nauru travel information
evidence.note: Official government information for visitors is delivered in English, supporting high traveler usability in English.

notes: Saint Helena should score English as fully compatible.

evidence.title: Saint Helena Tourism
evidence.note: Official tourism guidance is delivered in English for visitors.

evidence.title: Britannica: Saint Helena
evidence.note: Britannica describes Saint Helena as English-speaking.

notes: Svalbard and Jan Mayen should score Norwegian as primary, with smaller Russian and English credit because of settlement patterns and tourism.

evidence.title: Visit Svalbard
evidence.note: Official tourism guidance is delivered in English for visitors to Svalbard.

evidence.title: Britannica: Svalbard and Jan Mayen
evidence.note: Britannica explains the Norwegian administration of the territory and the presence of Russian-speaking settlements in Svalbard.

notes: Sierra Leone should score English as official and Krio as the most practical everyday lingua franca.

evidence.note: Britannica identifies English as the official language of Sierra Leone.

evidence.note: Britannica explains that Krio functions as the country's main lingua franca.

notes: San Marino should score Italian as fully compatible.

evidence.note: Britannica identifies Italian as the language of San Marino.

evidence.note: Official tourism guidance supports Italian as the core local language while also serving international visitors.

notes: Senegal should score French as official and Wolof as the strongest everyday lingua franca.
